lines: Find a comfortable position for sleep. [long pause] Adjust whatever needs adjusting — your pillow, your blanket, the angle of your body. [long pause] Shift your hips until the mattress holds you evenly. [long pause] Tuck the blanket around you if that feels good. [long pause] Make it right for you. [long pause] There's no wrong way to do this.

Notice the places where your body meets the bed. [long pause] The weight of your head on the pillow. [long pause] Your back pressed gently against the mattress. [long pause] Your legs settling into place, heavy and still. [long pause] These contact points are your anchors for tonight. [long pause] Feel each one. [long pause] You are supported. [long pause] You are held up by something solid.

Let your shoulders drop away from your ears. [long pause] They may have been creeping up all day without you noticing. [long pause] Let them fall now. [long pause] An inch. [long pause] Then another. [long pause] Let your hands unclench — you might not have realized they were clenched. [long pause] Let your jaw release so your lips part just slightly. [long pause] Let your tongue go soft in your mouth. [long pause] Let the muscles around your eyes loosen.

Take a slow breath in through your nose... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] filling your chest and your belly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and let it go through your mouth. [long pause] A long, warm, releasing exhale.
Again. [long pause] Slow breath in... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and let it pour out of you like water from a tilted cup.

One more. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out. [long pause] Let the breathing settle into whatever rhythm feels natural. [long pause] You don't need to manage it. [long pause] Your body knows how to breathe.

Tonight we practice a form of prayer that has roots in both contemplative Christian tradition and ancient Buddhist practice — loving-kindness. [long pause] In some traditions it's called metta. [long pause] In Christian contemplative prayer, it echoes the great commandment Jesus repeated more than any other: love one another. [long pause] Love your neighbor as yourself. [long pause] Love even your enemies.

We'll move through widening circles of compassion — starting with yourself, then someone you love, then someone neutral, then someone difficult, and finally... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] everyone. [long pause] All beings. [long pause] The whole broken, beautiful world.
The apostle John writes: "There is no fear in love. [long pause] Perfect love casts out fear." First John, chapter four, verse eighteen.

Fear and love occupy the same territory in the body — the tight chest, the clenched hands, the guarded heart. [long pause] Tonight, we let love expand into those places where fear has been living. [long pause] Not by force. [long pause] By invitation.

Place a hand on your heart if that feels comfortable. [long pause] If it doesn't, just rest your hand on your belly or at your side. [long pause] Feel the rhythm underneath your palm — that faithful beat that has accompanied you since before you drew your first breath. [long pause] It has never taken a break. [long pause] It has never abandoned its post. [long pause] Even now, it works for you while you rest.

We begin with you. [long pause] This is often the hardest part. [long pause] Most of us find it easier to extend kindness to a stranger than to direct it inward. [long pause] We have been trained to deflect. [long pause] To minimize. [long pause] To say "I'm fine" when we're not. [long pause] If these words feel awkward directed at yourself — that's common. [long pause] You're not performing. [long pause] You're not trying to convince yourself of anything. [long pause] You're practicing receiving what God already gives freely.
On each exhale, let these phrases settle into your body. [long pause] Not as commands. [long pause] Not as affirmations you're supposed to believe. [long pause] As offerings. [long pause] As seeds.
May I be healthy and whole.
May I be healthy and whole.
May I be healthy and whole.
May I know that I am loved... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] as I am.
May I know that I am loved... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] as I am.
May I know that I am loved... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] as I am.

Sit with that for a moment. [long pause] You are loved as you are. [long pause] Not a cleaned-up version. [long pause] Not a future version. [long pause] Not the version that finally has it together. [long pause] This version. [long pause] Tonight. [long pause] With all of today still clinging to you — the failures, the frustrations, the things you wish you'd done differently. [long pause] Loved. [long pause] Not in spite of those things. [long pause] Through them.

Now bring to mind someone you love. [long pause] A person whose face makes you soften when you think of them. [long pause] It could be a partner, a child, a parent, a friend, someone living or someone gone. [long pause] See their face clearly. [long pause] The way they smile. [long pause] The sound of their voice. [long pause] Feel the warmth that rises in your chest when you think of them — that effortless, uncomplicated love.
Hold them in your mind and offer these same words. [long pause] As naturally as breathing.
May you be healthy and whole.
May you be healthy and whole.
May you be healthy and whole.
May you know that you are loved.
May you know that you are loved.
May you know that you are loved.

Now let that person's face gently fade, and bring to mind someone neutral. [long pause] Someone you see regularly but don't really know. [long pause] The cashier at the grocery store. [long pause] A neighbor you pass on the sidewalk. [long pause] A face from your commute. [long pause] Someone whose name you might not know, whose story you've never heard.

This person has a life as complex as yours. [long pause] They have people who love them. [long pause] They have worries that keep them up at night. [long pause] They have a favorite song and a recurring fear and a memory that makes them laugh when no one's watching. [long pause] They, too, were made in the image of God. [long pause] They carry the same divine fingerprint as you.
May you be healthy and whole.
May you know that you are loved.
Again, slowly. [long pause] Let each phrase breathe.
May you be safe. [long pause] May you be at peace. [long pause] May you be healthy and whole. [long pause] May you know that you are loved.

Now — and this is the stretch — bring to mind someone difficult. [long pause] Not the most painful person in your life if that feels like too much tonight. [long pause] But someone who frustrates you. [long pause] Someone you're irritated with. [long pause] Someone who let you down, or spoke carelessly, or made your life harder. [long pause] Let their face come to mind. [long pause] If it brings tension, notice that. [long pause] You don't have to push through it. [long pause] Just notice.

This is not about approval. [long pause] You're not saying what they did was okay. [long pause] You're not erasing what happened. [long pause] You're not pretending it didn't hurt. [long pause] You are simply acknowledging that this person, too, is a human being. [long pause] Made of the same fragile, confused, sometimes selfish stuff as you. [long pause] Carrying their own wounds, their own fears, their own history that you may never understand.

Jesus said love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you. [long pause] Not because they deserve it — maybe they don't. [long pause] Because the alternative is carrying bitterness to bed, and bitterness is a weight that only hurts the one holding it.
If you can — and it's okay if this feels thin, or reluctant, or barely whispered — offer these words.
May you find healing for whatever drives you to cause pain.
May you, too, know that you are loved by God.

If that was hard, let it go now. [long pause] You don't have to mean it perfectly. [long pause] You don't have to feel warm and fuzzy. [long pause] You just have to be willing. [long pause] The willingness is the prayer. [long pause] God can work with willingness.

Now open the circle as wide as it can go. [long pause] Let it expand beyond this room, beyond this street, beyond this city. [long pause] Every person lying awake tonight. [long pause] Every child afraid of the dark, pulling the covers up over their head. [long pause] Every parent staring at the ceiling, wondering how to pay for tomorrow. [long pause] Every lonely soul in an empty room. [long pause] Every person in a hospital bed. [long pause] Every person sleeping on concrete. [long pause] Every person who has no one to say these words to them.
May all beings be at peace.
May all beings be healthy and whole.
May all beings know that they are loved.
"We love because He first loved us." First John, chapter four, verse nineteen.

You don't generate this love from your own reserves. [long pause] You would run dry in a day. [long pause] It flows from God through you — an infinite current passing through a willing vessel. [long pause] You don't have to produce anything tonight. [long pause] Just stay open. [long pause] Just let it move through you the way breath moves through you — without effort, without earning it, without end.

God, I don't always love well. [long pause] Some days I barely love at all. [long pause] I get tired. [long pause] I get selfish. [long pause] I close my hands when I should open them. [long pause] But tonight I opened my hands and tried. [long pause] I offered what I could — to myself, to the people I love, to the strangers, even to the difficult ones. [long pause] Take these thin, imperfect offerings and multiply them the way you do with bread and fish. [long pause] Fill the gaps where my love falls short with yours. [long pause] It's enough. [long pause] It's always been enough. [long pause] Amen.
Now let the words go. [long pause] Let the faces go. [long pause] Let the effort go.

Feel your whole body softening. [long pause] Like wax near a flame — not burning, just... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] releasing its shape. [long pause] The hard edges of the day melting away. [long pause] Your boundaries becoming gentle. [long pause] Your defenses lowering.
Your feet are heavy. [long pause] So heavy. [long pause] Your calves are heavy. [long pause] Your thighs sink deeper into the mattress with each exhale.

Your belly is soft — genuinely soft. [long pause] No holding, no bracing. [long pause] Your chest rises and falls with an easy, unhurried rhythm. [long pause] Your shoulders have nothing left to carry.
Your arms are heavy. [long pause] Your hands are open and still. [long pause] Your fingers rest, curled gently, holding nothing at all.
Your neck releases. [long pause] Your jaw is slack. [long pause] Your face is smooth and calm and still. [long pause] Like a sleeping child's face. [long pause] Unguarded. [long pause] Unthreatened.

You are loved. [long pause] And tonight, you loved. [long pause] You extended kindness in every direction — inward, outward, even toward the difficult. [long pause] That is more than enough for one day.
Rest now. [long pause] Let love carry you the rest of the way.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
